cards.title: Apple Vision Pro Exec Joins OpenAI Hardware Team
cards.body: Paul Meade, Apple's VP overseeing Vision Pro, is reportedly departing to join OpenAI's hardware division. The move signals OpenAI's accelerated push into physical AI devices.

cards.title: Trump Admin Authorizes Anthropic Mythos Use for 100+ Orgs
cards.body: Over 100 U.S. companies and government agencies now have authorized access to Mythos 5, including non-American employees. The clearance marks a major policy shift on AI model distribution.

cards.title: Asian Startups Launch Mythos-Rival Models to Bypass Export Ban
cards.body: New AI models launching across Asia promise Mythos-equivalent capabilities without U.S. export restrictions. The trend could permanently shift AI market dominance away from American labs.

cards.title: OpenAI Limits GPT-5.6 Rollout After Government Request
cards.body: OpenAI restricted GPT-5.6 distribution following Trump administration pressure, citing concerns about limiting access to critical users. The company warns that government vetting shouldn't become standard practice.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches Jalapeño Custom Inference Chip
cards.body: OpenAI unveiled Jalapeño, a custom inference chip built with Broadcom to reduce Nvidia dependence. The move joins Google, Apple, and SpaceX in building proprietary AI silicon.

cards.title: OpenAI Poaches Uber India Chief for Expansion
cards.body: OpenAI hired India's former Uber leader to head its largest non-U.S. market. The appointment accelerates the company's office expansion and partnership growth in India.

cards.title: White House Requests OpenAI Slow GPT-5.6 Release
cards.body: The Trump administration asked OpenAI to limit GPT-5.6 availability to select partners over safety concerns. The restricted rollout marks an unprecedented government intervention in AI model launches.

cards.title: Patronus AI Raises $50M for AI Agent Testing Platform
cards.body: The agent-testing startup, founded by ex-Meta researchers, secured $50M amid surging demand for stress-testing AI systems. Patronus creates digital environments to validate AI agent reliability.

cards.title: Claude Gains Ground Against ChatGPT in Paid Market
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning over paying consumers despite ChatGPT's dominant market position. Paid user data shows a notable shift in premium AI adoption patterns.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $320M for Game-Trained AI Agents
cards.body: The startup raised $320M to scale AI trained on millions of hours of video game footage. The company believes gameplay data can help AI develop human-like intuition.

cards.title: Databricks Former Chief Promises 1000x Power Reduction
cards.body: Un-0, an image-generation tool, demonstrates how Databricks' technology can replicate conventional AI systems with dramatically lower power consumption. The breakthrough could reshape AI infrastructure economics.

cards.title: Netris Raises $15M Series A for Neocloud Networks
cards.body: a16z-backed Netris secured $15M for software that accelerates neocloud operator launches. The platform reduces deployment time via intelligent network switching solutions.

cards.title: Adobe Acquires Video Enhancement Firm Topaz Labs
cards.body: Adobe announced its acquisition of Topaz Labs, a maker of image and video enhancement tools. The tools will integrate across Adobe's creative applications.

cards.title: Amazon Invests $13B in India AI Infrastructure Expansion
cards.body: Amazon announced fresh $13B investment in Indian AI infrastructure as global tech companies compete for market dominance. The commitment reflects the strategic importance of India's growing AI sector.

cards.title: Apple Vision Pro exec joins OpenAI hardware team
cards.body: Paul Meade, Apple's VP in charge of Vision Pro, is reportedly leaving to join OpenAI's hardware division. The move signals OpenAI's push into physical AI devices.

cards.title: Founder uses Claude AI to fight cancer diagnosis
cards.body: Connor Christou fed blood results, scan data, and wearables into Claude to help manage his cancer treatment. The AI helped him process and analyze critical health information.

cards.title: Asian startups launch models amid Anthropic export ban
cards.body: New AI models launching in Asia promise capabilities comparable to Anthropic's Mythos without export restrictions. U.S. AI labs risk losing the Asian market.

cards.title: Trump administration authorizes Mythos for 100+ companies
cards.body: Over 100 U.S. companies and government agencies gain access to Anthropic's Mythos 5 model, including non-American employees. The authorization expands AI adoption in government and enterprise.

cards.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request
cards.body: OpenAI restricted GPT-5.6 distribution following a government access request, but argues the process shouldn't become standard. The company says restrictions keep best tools from users and defenders.

cards.title: OpenAI hires Uber India chief for expansion
cards.body: OpenAI poaches Uber India's leadership to expand its presence in India's major market. The hire marks aggressive investment in offices, partnerships, and local talent.

cards.title: Tech giants build custom AI chips to challenge Nvidia
cards.body: OpenAI, Google, Apple, and SpaceX are building proprietary AI chips to reduce Nvidia dependence. OpenAI's Jalapeño inference chip, built with Broadcom, signals the shift away from chip market dominance.

cards.title: AI model capabilities now have political consequences
cards.body: Advanced AI models like those from Anthropic and OpenAI require collective action to manage real-world political impacts. The competition between AI labs has evolved beyond business rivalry.

cards.title: OpenAI's Jalapeño chip challenges Nvidia's dominance
cards.body: OpenAI's custom Jalapeño inference chip, built with Broadcom, marks Big Tech's biggest move away from Nvidia. The chip market's total dependence on Nvidia is ending.

cards.title: White House asks OpenAI to slow GPT-5.6 release
cards.body: OpenAI plans limited GPT-5.6 rollout to select partners at the Trump administration's request over safety concerns. The government intervention sets a precedent for AI model releases.

cards.title: Patronus AI raises $50M for AI agent testing
cards.body: Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, landed $50M to build digital worlds for stress-testing AI agents. Investor demand for agent validation tools is nearly insatiable.

cards.title: Claude gains ground on ChatGPT in paid subscriptions
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning over paid consumers despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead. Data shows a shift in consumer preference toward Claude among subscription users.

cards.title: General Intuition raises $320M for gameplay-trained AI
cards.body: General Intuition secured $320M funding to scale AI trained on millions of hours of video game footage. The startup bets that action data from games can develop human-like AI intuition.

cards.title: Databricks AI chief seeks 1,000x power reduction
cards.body: Un-0 is an image-generation tool showing how Databricks' technology can replicate conventional AI systems with dramatic efficiency gains. The breakthrough could cut AI's power consumption dramatically.

cards.title: Netris raises $15M to accelerate neocloud launches
cards.body: Netris raised $15M Series A from a16z to help AI neoclouds go live faster with network software. The platform reduces deployment time for cloud infrastructure operators.

cards.title: Adobe acquires Topaz Labs for enhancement tools
cards.body: Adobe acquired image and video enhancement maker Topaz Labs and plans integration across its creative suite. The acquisition expands Adobe's AI-powered content tools.

cards.title: Amazon invests $13B more in India AI infrastructure
cards.body: Amazon doubled down on India with a fresh $13B AI infrastructure investment. The move reflects global tech competition to expand AI capabilities in emerging markets.

cards.title: OpenAI restrictions reshape AI model access landscape
cards.body: Unprecedented government restrictions on OpenAI's GPT-5.6 signal a new era of AI regulation. The limitations highlight growing political consequences of advanced AI capabilities.

cards.title: AI reshapes retail through behind-the-scenes decisions
cards.body: Artificial intelligence is transforming retail not through flashy consumer features but through backend decision-making systems. AI controls product surfacing, inventory management, and customer targeting.

cards.title: IBM chip extends Moore's Law another decade
cards.body: IBM built a prototype chip with 100 billion transistors in fingernail-size area, doubling previous density. The breakthrough could enable faster, more energy-efficient AI systems for years.

cards.title: Stripe, Anthropic, OpenAI fund respiratory infection research
cards.body: Stripe, Anthropic, and OpenAI are funding research to prevent respiratory infections like the common cold. The collaboration combines AI expertise with biomedical research.

cards.title: Web data infrastructure emerges for AI scaling
cards.body: Enterprises need unstructured web data at scale to maximize AI potential, creating demand for new data infrastructure. This layer bridges blocked information and AI model requirements.

cards.title: NYT sues Microsoft for copyright-infringing AI supercomputer
cards.body: The New York Times accused Microsoft of building a copyright-infringing supercomputer to train OpenAI models. The lawsuit highlights ongoing AI training data disputes.

cards.title: Notion kills email app as users prefer AI agents
cards.body: Notion discontinued its Skiff-influenced email app because most users switched to AI agent alternatives. The move reflects changing preferences for AI-powered communication tools.

cards.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of massive Claude cloning
cards.body: Anthropic claims Alibaba conducted the largest Claude model cloning attack and demands punishment. The incident represents a major AI security and IP violation.
